description: A plain screenshot of a news article's headline and standfirst/subhead on a white background, with no agency design, seal, logo or graphic treatment. The bold dark sans-serif headline reads 'Trade school enrollment for young adults on the rise nationwide' and the lighter-gray subhead beneath reads 'One in three adults recommending trade school over a four-year university to high school grads.' Because it is only cropped article text with no designed overlay or branding, it is not an agency-designed meme/poster. The Department of Labor uses it to support the tweet's celebratory framing ('The Golden Age of SKILLED TRADES!') promoting vocational/skilled-trade careers over four-year college. Marked is_designed=false (headline screenshot).
notable_text: Trade school enrollment for young adults on the rise nationwide | One in three adults recommending trade school over a four-year university to high school grads
